title: Exohebea angusta

It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Exohebea angusta include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Exohebea is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
